jokes.text: Get to Matlock! Matlock!
jokes.setup: Dignitary trying to give a serious health care speech
jokes.punchline: Elderly audience only cares about seeing TV's Matlock
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural commentary about elderly priorities, economically delivered interruption

jokes.text: I give you the man who puts young people behind bars... Where they belong-- TV's Matlock!
jokes.setup: Expected typical celebrity introduction
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly sinister endorsement of imprisoning youth
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of typical celebrity intro with unexpectedly fascist sentiment

jokes.text: swiped his pills... 'Take one every hour to prevent spastic heart convulsions.'
jokes.setup: Grampa seems depressed after meeting his hero
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation he stole Matlock's heart medication
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - casual criminality from elderly fan, absurd specificity of medical condition

jokes.text: Wait, that's my hand.
jokes.setup: Grampa feeling death's clammy hand on his shoulder
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's just his own hand
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and setup/payoff structure, great character moment for senile Grampa

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson. Stop. Your constant letters are becoming a nuisance. Stop. If you do not cease, I will be forced to pursue legal action. Stop. Signed, Boris Karloff. Hollywood, California.
jokes.setup: Grampa giving Lisa his 'personal correspondence'
jokes.punchline: It's a cease and desist letter from Boris Karloff in telegram format
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation about Grampa as obsessive fan, formal telegram language adds absurdity

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! ... D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer excited to receive inheritance
jokes.punchline: Deflation when he learns it's for 'entire family'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer emotional whiplash, perfect character integration with signature catchphrases

jokes.text: You see, back in those days, rich men would ride around in zeppelins, dropping coins on people.
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining how he got the silver dollars
jokes.punchline: Completely fictional version of early 20th century economics
jokes.explanation: Peak Grampa storytelling - absurd historical revisionism delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Dad, we'd love to stay here and listen to your amusing 'antidotes,' but we have to take these coins to the mall and spend 'em!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to politely excuse the family from Grampa's stories
jokes.punchline: Says 'antidotes' instead of 'anecdotes'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism - sounds polite while being completely wrong

jokes.text: I just used it that morning to wash my turkey... which in those days was known as a 'walking bird.'
jokes.setup: Grampa continuing his rambling story about the washtub
jokes.punchline: Claims turkeys used to be called 'walking birds'
jokes.explanation: More Grampa historical revisionism, 'walking bird' is absurdly literal renaming

jokes.text: Cranberries, 'injun eyes,' and yams stuffed with gunpowder.
jokes.setup: Describing old-timey Thanksgiving dinner
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and dangerous traditional foods
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity culminating in explosive stuffing, great character voice

jokes.text: Then we'd all watch football, which in those days was called 'baseball.'
jokes.setup: More nostalgic reminiscing
jokes.punchline: Claims football and baseball had opposite names
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa logic - completely backwards historical 'facts' delivered with authority

jokes.text: D'oh! [electrical buzzing and explosion from dangerous toy]
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Marge's safety concerns about baby toy
jokes.punchline: Toy immediately proves dangerous by shocking/exploding on Homer
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate comeuppance, toy danger becomes literal instantly

jokes.text: When I was young, toys were built to last. Look at this junk! It breaks the first time you take it out of the box.
jokes.setup: Grampa complaining about modern toy quality
jokes.punchline: While literally breaking toys as he examines them
jokes.explanation: Self-fulfilling complaint - Grampa's criticism becomes literally true through his actions

jokes.text: And look at these toy soldiers! They'll break the second I step on 'em. [proceeds to step on them]
jokes.setup: Grampa predicting toy soldiers will break if stepped on
jokes.punchline: Immediately steps on them to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to previous joke, Grampa's destructive 'testing' continues the pattern

jokes.text: [Rock Around the Clock performance followed by immediate booing]
jokes.setup: Homer performing enthusiastically on toy piano
jokes.punchline: Audience immediately boos his performance
jokes.explanation: Simple setup/payoff of Homer's overconfidence meeting reality

jokes.text: Hey, horse face! Get your ugly pie-hooks off that Summer Fun Set!
jokes.setup: Marge warning Lisa she might get crazy shopping for Malibu Stacy
jokes.punchline: Immediately becomes aggressively territorial over toys
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion - sweet Marge becomes vicious toy shopper instantly, 'pie-hooks' is wonderfully specific

jokes.text: Look! Achy Breaky Stacy for $1.99! [Gasps] Live from the Improv Stacy's only 89 cents. [Both] Ew!
jokes.setup: Mother and daughter excited about discount Stacy dolls
jokes.punchline: Both equally disgusted by the terrible variety names
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire of 90s trends applied to toys, shared disgust reaction is well-timed

jokes.text: Marge, that's what I bought it for. [proceeds to use missile launcher while driving]
jokes.setup: Marge questioning if Homer should use toy while driving
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact justification for dangerous behavior
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer logic - completely unsafe behavior justified by simple reasoning

jokes.text: My skull is eggshell-thin.
jokes.setup: Grampa warning Homer to be careful with toy weapons
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific medical overshare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa overshare - absurdly specific and disturbing medical detail delivered casually

jokes.text: There sure are a lot of ugly people in your neighborhood.
jokes.setup: First words from talking Malibu Stacy doll
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly mean-spirited social observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expected sweet doll dialogue with harsh social judgment

jokes.text: The president is a demi-crat!
jokes.setup: Continuing inappropriate doll commentary
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Democrat' as 'demi-crat'
jokes.explanation: Good political satire with childish mispronunciation adding to the absurdity

jokes.text: Hello? I can't unbuckle my seat belt. Hello?!
jokes.setup: Doll continuing to speak
jokes.punchline: Desperately calling for help with basic car safety
jokes.explanation: Escalating helplessness from fashion doll, building the pattern of inappropriate statements

jokes.text: Dad, I love you. But... you're a weird, sore-headed old crank, and nobody likes you!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to comfort his father with loving words
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty immediately following the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - combines genuine affection with devastating honesty, great emotional whiplash

jokes.text: Holy smokes! That's it! From now on, I'm thinkin', actin' and lookin' young.
jokes.setup: Grampa inspired by youth-oriented Buzz Cola commercial
jokes.punchline: Elderly man deciding to completely reinvent himself based on soda ad
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Grampa's subplot, naive susceptibility to advertising is very character-appropriate

jokes.text: Oh! Ow! Ow! The bubbles are burning my tongue! Ow! Ooh. Water! Water!
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to act young with trendy soda
jokes.punchline: Immediately can't handle carbonation due to age
jokes.explanation: Immediate physical comedy payoff to Grampa's youth ambitions, age catching up instantly

jokes.text: I wish they taught shopping in school!
jokes.setup: Lisa waiting to hear meaningful words from her doll
jokes.punchline: Vacuous anti-intellectual statement about priorities
jokes.explanation: Sharp feminist critique - doll promotes shopping over education, perfect setup for Lisa's feminist awakening

jokes.text: Don't ask me. I'm just a girl! [Giggles]
jokes.setup: Lisa asking doll for something meaningful to say
jokes.punchline: Doll deflects with self-deprecating gender stereotype
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted feminist critique - economical, devastating, and becomes the episode's central thesis statement

jokes.text: That they can never be anything more than vacuous ninnies whose only goal is to look pretty, land a rich husband and spend all day on the phone with their equally vacuous friends talking about how damn terrific it is to look pretty and have a rich husband!
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining the problem with Malibu Stacy's messaging
jokes.punchline: Passionate feminist rant with escalating circular logic
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Lisa, builds the episode's feminist theme with passionate specificity

jokes.text: -Just what I was gonna say. - [Growls, Grunts]
jokes.setup: Lisa delivers passionate feminist speech
jokes.punchline: Bart pretends he was going to say the same thing, Marge growls in frustration
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamic - Bart's deflating joke and Marge's frustration with the situation

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm acting young! [proceeds to injure himself]
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to act youthful
jokes.punchline: Immediately hurts himself attempting young behavior
jokes.explanation: Continues Grampa's age vs. youth subplot with predictable physical comedy

jokes.text: [Male Voice] My Spidey sense is tingling. Anybody call for a web-slinger?
jokes.setup: Girl complaining her doll says wrong things
jokes.punchline: Doll has completely wrong voice and character (Spider-Man)
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist gag - wrong toy voice chip creates gender-crossed superhero moment

jokes.text: Lisa said a dirty word!
jokes.setup: Lisa using term 'sexist'
jokes.punchline: Kids think academic term is profanity
jokes.explanation: Good commentary on how children are sheltered from intellectual concepts but exposed to problematic messages. Shows the irony of what's considered inappropriate.

jokes.text: Yeah. You made us march in that Gay Rights Parade.
jokes.setup: Family discussing Lisa's activism
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of progressive political activity as burden
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's consistent activism, matter-of-fact delivery adds humor

jokes.text: And we can't watch Fox, 'cause they own those chemical weapon plants in Syria.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of Lisa's boycotts
jokes.punchline: Show making joke about its own network's fictional evil activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - show mocking its own network with absurd conspiracy theory

jokes.text: Now let's forget our troubles with a big bowl of strawberry ice cream. Now let's forget our troubles with a big bowl of strawberry ice cream!
jokes.setup: Marge dismissing Lisa's concerns with maternal comfort
jokes.punchline: Doll immediately repeats exact same vapid sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect feminist critique moment - shows how women internalize dismissive attitudes, brilliant parallel structure

jokes.text: Now let's forget our troubles with a big bowl of strawberry ice cream!
jokes.setup: Marge just said the exact same thing
jokes.punchline: Doll repeats identical phrase, proving Lisa's point about influence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural joke that proves Lisa's entire argument in one moment. The exact repetition is devastating evidence of the doll's influence on Marge. Perfect economy and impact.

jokes.text: Aw, crap. There's a clog in the torso chute! Leroy! Get your ass in gear. Shut your hole.
jokes.setup: Tour guide talking about magical toy production
jokes.punchline: Harsh factory reality immediately contradicts the fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of corporate PR vs. worker reality, undercuts the 'magical' factory tour

jokes.text: The dream? To mass-market a fashion doll that was also edible.
jokes.setup: Documentary about Stacy Lavelle's vision
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and impractical business concept
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist business concept, perfectly delivered as straight corporate history

jokes.text: Kids didn't much like the taste of dried onion meal, but they loved the doll.
jokes.setup: Explaining why edible doll failed
jokes.punchline: Understated explanation of obviously terrible food choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan follow-up, 'dried onion meal' is hilariously unappetizing for children

jokes.text: Hello, Malibu Stacy collectors. I'll see you at StacyCon '94, at the San Diego Airport Hilton.
jokes.setup: Introducing world's largest collector
jokes.punchline: Smithers' obsessive fandom extends to toy dolls, with sad convention location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers character moment - extends his obsessive personality to dolls, airport hotel adds patheticness

jokes.text: Hey, Jiggles! Grab a pad and back that gorgeous butt in here. Oh, you. Get away! Don't act like you don't like it.
jokes.setup: Executive claiming company is mindful of sexist concerns
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates blatant workplace sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical moment showing corporate hypocrisy about sexism, immediate contradiction is well-timed

jokes.text: I'm a white male, aged 18 to 49. Everyone listens to me, no matter how dumb my suggestions are.
jokes.setup: Various characters complaining about not being heard
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete lack of self-awareness about his demographic privilege
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on TV demographics and privilege, perfect Homer obliviousness, became quotable cultural reference

jokes.text: I'm a white male, aged 18 to 49. Everyone listens to me, no matter how dumb my suggestions are.
jokes.setup: Discussion of who gets heard in society
jokes.punchline: Homer perfectly describing TV demographic and his own privilege
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about TV demographics and social privilege. Homer's self-awareness is funny while making serious point about who gets attention. The specificity of the age range is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm gonna get me a job! A real Malibu and see if Stacy can help invent me young-- Help!
jokes.setup: Grampa inspired to get a job
jokes.punchline: Sentence dissolves into confused rambling ending in cry for help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Grampa's mental confusion and physical frailty

jokes.text: Come in? Come in! Mayday! I'm losing your transmission! - I said, 'French fries!'
jokes.setup: Grampa working at modern fast food job
jokes.punchline: Treats drive-thru headset like military radio communication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa character work - applying military experience to civilian job inappropriately. The contrast between dramatic radio protocol and mundane food order is excellent.

jokes.text: Do we sell French... fries? I-- Why, it's Homer Simpson's daughter.
jokes.setup: Smithers working fast food drive-through
jokes.punchline: Corporate executive confused by basic fast food menu
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - elite Smithers out of his element in service job

jokes.text: You're quite good... at turning... me on. Um, you probably should ignore that.
jokes.setup: Smithers working with computer
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sexual double entendre with awkward recovery
jokes.explanation: Typical Smithers sexual awkwardness, good character moment but predictable

jokes.text: All right! I've been waitin' nine years to get my Frisbee back.
jokes.setup: Stacy Lavelle's house being revealed
jokes.punchline: Random person excited about completely unrelated property recovery
jokes.explanation: Good random absurdist moment, shows neighborhood eccentric character

jokes.text: My name is Stacy. But you can call me-- [Wolf Whistle]
jokes.setup: Doll introducing herself formally
jokes.punchline: Name cuts off to sexualized sound effect
jokes.explanation: Sharp feminist critique - doll can't even state her name without being objectified

jokes.text: Well, that, and I was funneling profits to the Vietcong.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she was forced out of her company
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to treason as secondary reason
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - treats war crimes as minor detail, perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Five husbands: Ken, Johnny, Joe... Dr. Colossus, Steve Austin.
jokes.setup: Listing her failed marriages
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal names to supervillain and cyborg
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list joke, moves from mundane to fantastic, good Barbie reference parody

jokes.text: We need some more secret sauce! Put this mayonnaise in the sun.
jokes.setup: Grampa working at fast food restaurant
jokes.punchline: Reveals 'secret sauce' is just sun-heated mayonnaise
jokes.explanation: Good fast food satire - mysterious ingredients revealed as unsanitary shortcuts

jokes.text: Ever see a sandwich that could take a bite outta you? Look at the sandwich! It's gonna bite you!
jokes.setup: Grampa trying to be fun and youthful at work
jokes.punchline: Bizarre anthropomorphizing of dangerous sandwich
jokes.explanation: Grampa's misguided attempt at youth appeal, absurd but not especially clever

jokes.text: She'll have the wisdom of Gertrude Stein and the wit of Cathy Guisewite. The tenacity of Nina Totenberg and the common sense of Elizabeth Cady Stanton. And to top it off, the down-to-earth good looks of Eleanor Roosevelt.
jokes.setup: Describing ideal feminist doll
jokes.punchline: List escalates to hilariously unflattering physical description
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that subverts expectations, Eleanor Roosevelt reference is charmingly mean

jokes.text: I'll bomb your house into the ground, missy!
jokes.setup: Ex-husband trying to win Stacy back romantically
jokes.punchline: Immediately escalates to violent threats
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from romantic persistence to terrorism threat, shows why marriage failed

jokes.text: You all have hideous hair! I mean, from a design point of view.
jokes.setup: Simpson family offering to model for doll hair
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment with weak attempted save
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - honesty followed by insufficient damage control

jokes.text: Learn from a professional, kid. [Car door closing, tires screeching]
jokes.setup: Krusty showing off his professional voice work
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons the job after one take
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment - arrogant about minimal effort, then immediately leaves

jokes.text: 'Wendy Windbag'? 'Ugly Doris'? 'Hortense, the Mule-Faced Doll'?
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting doll names
jokes.punchline: Increasingly mean-spirited and specific insults
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Bart's crude humor, escalating insults work well

jokes.text: I can't stand this any longer. Somebody please pay attention to me! Hello! Pay attention to me. Look at me! I'm Bart, I'm Bart.
jokes.setup: Bart being ignored during serious doll planning
jokes.punchline: Complete meltdown demanding attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - can't handle not being the center of attention

jokes.text: Well, you were right about the Berlin Wall.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Lisa's news judgment
jokes.punchline: Admits she correctly predicted major historical event
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Homer's grudging admission of Lisa's superior judgment about world events

jokes.text: Oh, and the president was arrested for murder. More on that tomorrow night. Or you can turn to another channel. Oh. Do not turn to another channel.
jokes.setup: News anchor casually mentioning major story
jokes.punchline: Treats presidential arrest as throwaway, then desperately tries to retain viewers
jokes.explanation: Great media satire - prioritizes ratings over earth-shattering news, perfect character panic

jokes.text: The Good Lord lets us grow old for a reason. To gain the wisdom to find fault with everything he's made!
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining purpose of aging
jokes.punchline: Divine purpose is to become universally critical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical humor that captures elderly mindset perfectly. The religious framing of constant complaining is both insightful and funny.

jokes.text: And one more thing. I never once washed my hands. That's your policy, not mine.
jokes.setup: Grampa quitting his fast food job
jokes.punchline: Reveals disgusting hygiene violation as final insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect gross-out revelation for food service job, good character revenge moment

jokes.text: But she's got a new hat.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining that new Malibu Stacy is just cosmetic change
jokes.punchline: Girl completely misses the point, focuses on superficial addition
jokes.explanation: Perfect satirical moment about consumer gullibility, shows how easily people are fooled by cosmetic changes

jokes.text: Wait! Don't be fooled. She's just a regular Malibu Stacy with a stupid, cheap hat. She still embodies all the awful stereotypes she did before.
jokes.setup: New improved Malibu Stacy creating excitement
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out minimal cosmetic change doesn't address core problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment and brilliant satire of corporate responses to criticism. The 'stupid, cheap hat' detail captures exactly how businesses make meaningless changes.

jokes.text: You know, if we get through to just that one little girl... it'll all be worth it.
jokes.setup: Lisa finding silver lining in apparent failure
jokes.punchline: Classic idealistic teacher/activist consolation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's persistent idealism. The clichéd sentiment is both genuine and slightly naive.

jokes.text: Particularly if that little girl happens to pay $46,000 for that doll. What? Oh, nothing. Kudos to you, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Discussing making difference in one girl's life
jokes.punchline: Reveals mercenary motivation with awkward cover-up
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Stacy's continued commercial instincts despite feminist awakening
